mechanism_narrative: A significant year-over-year decline in housing starts (HOUST) indicates a severe contraction in construction and related industries, reducing aggregate demand and leading to downward revisions of future corporate earnings.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts had fallen sharply from their peak in early 2006 by the decision date.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when the trigger condition is met over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: After a prolonged tightening cycle, a high and stable federal funds rate constrains credit for corporations and consumers, slowing economic activity and compressing equity valuations as the effects of tight money permeate the economy.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and has been unchanged for at least 3 consecutive months.
evidence_refs: fact:The Federal Reserve held the Fed Funds rate at 5.25% from June 2006 through the decision date.
failure_condition: The information coefficient of the signal does not remain statistically negative (p < 0.10) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects underlying economic strength and robust corporate hiring, which supports higher equity prices through increased consumer spending and confidence in future earnings.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS showed consistent positive monthly changes throughout 2006.
failure_condition: The 3-month change in PAYEMS has a negative rank correlation with 20-day forward S&P 500 returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A deceleration in the year-over-year growth of industrial production (INDPRO) signals a slowdown in the manufacturing and goods-producing sectors, acting as a leading indicator for negative earnings revisions and lower stock prices.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO year-over-year growth falls below its 12-month moving average.
failure_condition: The signal fails to generate a statistically significant negative alpha in a walk-forward test over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate is low and inflation is above target, the central bank is constrained from easing policy. This interaction signals persistent tight monetary conditions, increasing the equity risk premium and negatively impacting returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Core inflation remained elevated in 2006, preventing the Fed from easing despite housing weakness.
failure_condition: In periods matching the trigger condition, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate reaching a cyclical low indicates maximum labor market tightness. This often precedes wage pressures, corporate margin compression, and the end of an economic expansion, leading to negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE is at or below its 24-month minimum.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.5% was near the cyclical lows for the 2002-2007 expansion.
failure_condition: The average 60-day forward return following the trigger is not statistically significantly lower than the unconditional average (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust near-term consumer demand, which translates directly into higher corporate revenues and supports positive short-term equity market performance.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the 1-month change in RSAFS and 5-day forward S&P 500 returns is less than 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth remains strong while the federal funds rate is already at a restrictive level, the market anticipates that monetary policy will remain tight to curb growth, leading to a higher discount rate and negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:The US economy was still growing at a solid pace in late 2006 while Fed policy was restrictive.
failure_condition: The strategy of shorting the S&P 500 when the trigger is active fails to produce a positive information ratio over a full business cycle.
